contribution.title: Riemannian geometry
contribution.description: Generalized Gauss's surface geometry to arbitrary dimensions, introducing the metric tensor and the Riemann curvature tensor.
contribution.relevance_to_blackhole_ds: The mathematical language of general relativity. Every spacetime metric in this project is a Riemannian (technically pseudo-Riemannian, Lorentzian) metric.

contribution.title: Riemann surfaces
contribution.description: Multi-sheeted surfaces on which multi-valued complex functions become single-valued.
contribution.relevance_to_blackhole_ds: Useful for visualizing complex coordinate systems in Kerr geometry.

reasoning_lens.proof_standard: Construct the right object for the question, then prove things about it.
reasoning_lens.favorite_question: What is the right space on which this object is well-defined?
How does curvature constrain what can happen here?
reasoning_lens.failure_mode: Riemann sometimes lacked formal rigor by modern standards; his arguments work but need cleaning.

reference.citation: Riemann, B. (1868). Uber die Hypothesen welche der Geometrie zu Grunde liegen. Abh. Gesellschaft Wiss. Gottingen 13.

how_to_apply.when_to_consult: Any time you reach for a metric tensor, curvature, or differential geometry.
how_to_apply.cautions: Mind the signature convention; Riemannian (positive definite) vs Lorentzian (one minus sign) matters for causality.
